title: Executive Order 13028—Further Amendments to Executive Order No. 12757—Implementation of the Enterprise for the Americas Initiative

flags.justification: A narrow, technical amendment updating statutory cross-references in an existing delegation framework to reflect new appropriations legislation, with a new provision requiring the Secretary to act in accordance with Council recommendations and in consultation with the Secretary of State.
Single purpose: updating an existing delegation order.
An internal delegation-maintenance amendment, not a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing delegation framework implementing permanent statutory authority.

Sec. 2 states this order is not intended to create any right or benefit enforceable by a party against the United States — a sixth confirmed instance of the no-private-right-of-action pattern in this corpus.
This concern is captured under Flag 7.
A narrow, technical amendment updating an existing delegation framework, not itself a novel authority claim.
The new provision requiring consultation with the Secretary of State is a well-designed coordination feature.
No new exemption category is created by this maintenance amendment.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/12 = 8.3%. A sixth confirmed instance of the no-private-right-of-action clause, alongside EO 12114, EO 12615, EO 12662, EO 12735, and EO 12999 — a well-established drafting convention spanning six presidencies' worth of subject matter.
